qas.q: Chaplain core three core competencies
qas.a: Nurture the living, care for the wounded, honor the fallen

qas.q: Why is expectant first in religious support triage
qas.a: Religious support is prioritized on basis of timely religious support intervention. These are casualties with very serious injuries and a low chance of survival

qas.q: Where do you get a religious denominational report from?
qas.a: S1 or AAA-339

qas.q: Who does the UMT coordinate with in the event of casualties?
qas.a: Hospital personnel, chaplain assistant, technical chain, and other religious support personnel

qas.q: Why is it important to have a good professional relationship with the medical training facility UMT?
qas.a: Helps identify placement, notifications, roles, and additional support.

qas.q: Who was the first religious affair specialist killed in action since 1970 and where?
qas.a: Staff sergeant Christopher T. Stout, Afghanistan

qas.q: Who is the highly decorated religious affair specialist?
qas.a: Cpl. Calvin P. Titus

qas.q: When did the MOS change 71B to 71M?
qas.a: August of 1965, when a change to AR 611-201 caused the MOS to change from 71B to 71M

qas.q: When was the religious affairs specialist was established and what was the general order number?
qas.a: The religious affairs specialist was established in general order #253 on 28 December 1909

qas.q: What unit additional duties will religious affairs specialist not support?
qas.a: Suicide prevention manager, casualty assistance officer, and master resiliency trainer

qas.q: What type of security clearance is required for entry into the MOS 56M
qas.a: Secret

qas.q: Religious affairs specialist apply their skills along three core capabilities. What are they?
qas.a: Integrate religious operations, spiritual fitness, basic human interaction

qas.q: Are religious affairs specialists required to support the unit and garrison CMRP?
qas.a: Yes

qas.q: When was the first unofficial school for religious affairs specialist's established and it's location?
qas.a: In 1933, the first unofficial school for religious affairs specialist started in San Antonio, Texas.

qas.q: When was the RAS mos change from 71M to 56M
qas.a: The MOS changed from 71M to 56M Oct. 1 2001

qas.q: Religious affair specialist can support additional duties that contribute to the welfare of command; however must consult with whom first?
qas.a: They must consult with their unit assigned chaplain before assigning duties

qas.q: What is the title of JG 1-05?
qas.a: Religious affairs in joint operation

qas.q: What is the basis of religious affairs?
qas.a: Us chaplains represent specific religious organizations and work together within the pluralistic context of the military to ensure freedom of religion

qas.q: In a joint operation, what is a unit ministry team called?
qas.a: Religious support team (RST)

qas.q: What is a memorial service
qas.a: It's religious in nature, voluntary attendance, the officiating Chaplain should represent the same faith group as the deceased

qas.q: What is a memorial service?
qas.a: It's patriotic in nature, attendance may be mandatory, command directs and led

qas.q: What is religious advisement
qas.a: The practice of informing the commander on the impact of religion on joint operations that include worship, rituals, protected places, customs and practices of us military personnel, international forces

qas.q: What is a religious support team compromised of?
qas.a: At least one chaplain and one enlisted RS person that works together. RST may come from the same or different service components

qas.q: Describe confidential communication
qas.a: Any communication given to a chaplain by an individual made either as a formal act of religion or as a matter of conscience

qas.q: What is the title of FM 1-05
qas.a: Religious support

qas.q: What is the title of AR 165-1
qas.a: Army chaplain corps activities
